meta.topic: 5 legal agreements every Indian founder must have before a deal blows up
meta.audience: First-time founders registering a Pvt Ltd or LLP in India

slides.headline: 5 agreements every Indian founder must have.
slides.subhead: Most founders discover the gap when a deal falls through.
That's all it takes for a co-founder dispute to freeze your company — bank accounts, domain, everything locked.

left.label: Without it
left.body: Partner stops working but keeps 50% equity. You can't sell, can't shut down, can't move on.

right.label: With it
right.body: Reverse vesting, buy-back price formula, and a dispute mechanism — defined before there's a dispute.

left.label: Without it
left.body: Domain, brand name, and codebase in a co-founder's personal name. They leave — your brand has no legal owner.

right.label: With it
right.body: All IP transferred to the company on day 1. Future employee and vendor work contractually assigned too.

slides.headline: The other 3: Privacy Policy (DPDP Act 2023 makes it mandatory). Terms of Service (defines your liability limits). Employment / Vendor NDA (work product stays with the company when people leave). None of these need to be expensive. They need to exist.
Comment AGREEMENTS below.
slides.body: I'll DM you the checklist we walk every new client through — all 5 documents with the specific clauses to include.

caption:
5 legal agreements every Indian founder must have — and most skip all of them.

The one that causes the most damage: the Co-Founder Agreement.

Your standard MOA won't cover what happens when a partner stops contributing but keeps 50% equity. You need:
→ Reverse vesting (what happens to their shares if they leave early)
→ Buy-back price formula (so there's no fight about valuation later)
→ Dispute resolution mechanism (so one disagreement doesn't deadlock the whole company)

The other four:
→ IP Assignment Agreement (domain + brand + code owned by the company, day 1)
→ Privacy Policy (DPDP Act 2023 makes this a legal requirement for Indian businesses collecting user data)
→ Terms of Service (defines what you're liable for — critical if you serve international clients)
→ Employment / Vendor NDA (work product belongs to the company when people leave)

None of these need to be expensive. They need to exist.

Comment AGREEMENTS below — I'll DM you the exact checklist we use at Envesty for every new client setup.

hooks: One missing clause can freeze your company's bank account. Here's which one.
Your co-founder agreement protects you from your co-founder — not just from the outside world.
